rulings.Case Title: ABRAHAM LINCOLN HIGH SCHOOL OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF EDUCATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause (Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 3/18/2021)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 6, 2021, Line 1, Order To Show Cause: The order to show cause is discharged, and the amended petition for writ of mandate is dismissed as moot. By the first amended petition, petitioners seek a writ of mandate ordering respondents to repeal Resolution No. 211-12A1, relating to the renaming of San Francisco Unified School District schools, as well as related declaratory and injunctive relief, on the grounds that in enacting that Resolution at its January 26, 2021 meeting, respondent Board of Education violated the Brown Act. On April 6, 2021, after suspending the work of the panel it had previously appointed to advise it regarding the renaming process, the Board rescinded Resolution 211-12A1. No school was renamed pursuant to the Board's resolutions. As a result, the parties agree that the proceeding is moot, and must be dismissed. "A case is considered moot when 'the question addressed was at one time a live issue in the case,' but has been deprived of life 'because of events occurring after the judicial process was initiated.' . . . . The pivotal question in determining if a case is moot is therefore whether the court can grant the plaintiff any effectual relief." (Wilson & Wilson v. City Council of Redwood City (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 1559, 1574 (citations omitted).) "When events render a case moot the court, whether trial or appellate, should generally dismiss it." (Id.; see, e.g., TransparentGov Novato v. City of Novato (2019) 34 Cal.App.5th 140, 150-153 [affirming dismissal of petition for violations of Brown Act, where city council adopted new policy following claimed violations, and petitioner therefore did not demonstrate a justifiable controversy warranting mandamus or declaratory relief].) The court declines petitioners' request in their late-filed reply to make findings that the action is not frivolous or to address any of the other arguments raised by the parties. "The rendering of advisory opinions falls within neither the functions nor the jurisdiction" of California courts. (People ex rel. Lynch v. Superior Court (1970) 1 Cal.3d 910, 912.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the respondents is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL A. VANDER WAERDT VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Initial And Further Responses To Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 6, 2021, Line 1, DEFENDANT JACK GARDNER's Motion To Compel Initial And Further Responses To Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Sanctions in the amount of $500 awarded to Defendant. Supplemental responses to be served by May 28, 2021. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 6, 2021, Line 2, Plaintiffs Minsoo Kim And Young Steven Kim's Motion For Terminating Sanctions: Plaintiffs' motion for terminating sanctions as to Defendant InstQuest is granted. InstQuest has not filed an opposition to the motion. InstQuest is a corporation that has been unrepresented by counsel since October 28, 2020, after this case was remanded to this court after InstQuest refused to post arbitration fees necessary to allow the arbitration previously ordered by the court to proceed. Since that remand, InstQuest has failed to appear for a noticed deposition, and has ignored communications from Plaintiffs' counsel. The Court will strike Defendant's answer to the complaint. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1281.99(b).) Plaintiffs thereafter may file a request for default and seek entry of default judgment in accordance with Local Rule 6.6. rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice And Motion For Request For Reconsideration Of New Evidence Under Ccp 1008; Declaration And Exhibits A-C
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 6, 2021, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CATHERINE WATSON's Motion For Request For Reconsideration Of New Evidence Under CCP 1008. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. Per CCP 1008(a), the motion for reconsideration of various prior discovery orders is untimely. The last order referenced by Plaintiff was a motion for terminating sanctions. The order on that motion was served by the Court on March 22, 2021. Any motion for reconsideration was due on April 6, 2021 (10 days + 5 days for mail). This motion was filed on April 7, 2021. The other orders referenced by Plaintiff were issued weeks or months before April 7, 2021. Moreover, even if the motions were timely, Plaintiff has failed to provide evidence establishing new or different facts, circumstances, or law as required by CCP 1008(a). As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ESSEX PORTFOLIO, LP VS. SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 6, 2021, Line 10, DEFENDANT MVE + PARTNERS, INC.'s DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT: Defendant MVE + Partners (MVEP)'s demurrer to the seventh cause of action for negligence is overruled. "An omission to perform a contract obligation is never a tort, unless that omission is also an omission of a legal duty." (Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 551, 554.) "While Erlich states that a claimant is required to plead a tort duty independent of the contract, Erlich does not address whether improper performance of a professional services contract gives rise to an independent tort duty. . . . Cases decided subsequent to . . . Erlich continue to apply a rule that negligent failure to exercise reasonable care and skill in undertaking to perform a professional services contract is a tort as well as a breach of contract." (City and County of San Francisco v. Cambridge Integrated Services Group, Inc. (N.D. Cal., July 2, 2007, No. C 04-1523 VRW) 2007 WL 1970092, at *3-*4, citing several cases cited below. Defendant's citation to and reliance upon an unpublished Court of Appeal decision (Michaels) in its moving papers (Mot. at 3:6-4:6) is improper. (Cal. R. Ct. 8.1115(a) ["an opinion of a California Court of Appeal . . . that is not certified for publication or ordered published must not be cited or relied on by a court or a party in any other action"].) An architect has a professional duty of care. (See Montijo v. Swift (1963) 219 Cal.App.2d 351, 353 ["an architect who plans and supervises construction work, as an independent contractor, is under a duty to exercise ordinary care in the course thereof for the protection of any person who foreseeably and with reasonable certainty may be injured by his failure to do so"].) There is a long line of California cases establishing that professional negligence may sound in both tort and contract. (See Gagne v. Bertran (1954) 43 Cal.2d 481, 490 [soil tester]; Moreno v. Sanchez (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 1415, 1436 [home inspector]; Neel v. Magana, Olney, Levy, Cathcart & Gelfand (1971) 6 Cal.3d 176, 180-181 [attorney]; Loube v. Loube (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 421, 429- 430 [attorney].) Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a claim for professional negligence. "The elements of a cause of action for professional negligence are: (1) the duty of the professional to use such skill, prudence and diligence as other members of the profession commonly possess and exercise; (2) breach of that duty; (3) a causal connection between the negligent conduct and the resulting injury; and (4) actual loss or damage resulting from the professional negligence." (Loube, 64 Cal.App.4th at 429.) "Defendants . . . were under a duty to exercise ordinary care as . . . architects . . . and . . . failed to perform their duty. . . . As a direct and proximate result of the foregoing negligent, careless, and unworkmanlike actions and/or omissions by Defendants, and each of them, Plaintiff has suffered damages." (FAC 86-88.) [The Court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties.] =(302/EPS)

The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Nonparty Indoor Restore Environmental Services was previously held in contempt for failing to comply with Defendants' subpoena, however, the Court stated that the contempt finding would be discharged if Indoor Restore complied with the subpoena by the last hearing date. Indoor Restore is required to pay all sanctions and comply with the subpoena by May 21, 2021. The Court will hold another hearing on June 3, 2021 in Department 301 at 9:00 am. Indoor Restore is ordered to appear on that day and confirm that all sanctions have been paid and the subpoena complied with. rulings.Case Title: MEAGAN MIELKE VS. JEFREY KIRSCHENBAUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion And Motion To Disqualify Arbitrator; **Court Reporter Fees Paid**
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 6, 2021, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MEAGAN MIELKE's Motion To Disqualify Arbitrator: Plaintiff's motion to disqualify arbitrator, which plaintiff filed without first seeking to lift the stay, is denied without prejudice. On November 18, 2019, this Court granted defendants' petition to compel arbitration of this professional negligence action and stayed the litigation. The matter was referred for arbitration to the Hon. James Lambden (Ret.) at ADR Services, Inc. Since that time, the parties have held a number of prehearing conferences, conducted discovery, and the arbitrator has issued a number of written rulings. Before the instant motion arose, the final arbitration hearing was scheduled to be held on April 26-30, 2021. Plaintiff has a variety of complaints about how Justice Lambden has conducted the arbitration proceedings, including his rulings on a number of scheduling and substantive issues, which she contends call his impartiality into question. ADR Services, Inc. Arbitration Rules, Rule 11(E) allows a party, "[a]t any time before the conclusion of the arbitration proceeding," to exercise the arbitrator on any of the grounds listed in Code of Civil Procedure section 170.1. In response to plaintiff's demand that he disqualify himself, the arbitrator declined to do so, stating, "I know of no facts or circumstances which would require my disqualification or recusal in this case." Nevertheless, on February 1, 2021, the arbitrator issued Arbitration Order #5 allowing plaintiff 30 days to move this Court for an order determining whether the arbitration should proceed before him, or whether a new arbitrator should be selected. The order was issued pursuant to ADR Services Rule 12, which states in pertinent part, "Upon objection of a party to the continued service of a neutral arbitrator, ADR Services shall refer the matter to the Court for determination, which shall be conclusive." In the same order, the arbitrator also vacated the continued arbitration hearing, and temporarily stayed the arbitration proceeding pending this Court's decision. [The Court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties.] As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 6, 2021, Line 8, 3. PLAINTIFFS JOSEFINA MCGARRY (as an individual), JOSEFINA MCGARRY (as Successor In Interest for Stella Grace Yeh) and MCKENNA LIMENTANI'S (as Successor In Interest for Stella Grace Yeh) Motion To Compel Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Rasier, LLC, And Rasier-Ca LLC, Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Rasier, LLC, And Rasier-Ca LLC And Defendants Attorney, Justina L. Tate, In The Amount Of $11,940.00: The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel defendants' responses to requests for production of documents is granted in part and denied in part as set forth in the report and recommendation. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. Defendants shall serve complete and verified further responses, and provide all relevant documents, within 10 court days of the court's order. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: 640 OCTAVIA LLC ET AL VS. GREGORY S. WALSTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Gregory S. Walston And Walston And Associates Dba The Walston Law Groups, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses From Plaintiffs 640 Octavia Llc And Edward Kountze
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 6, 2021, Line 7, Defendant Gregory S. Walston and Walston and Associates dba The Walston Law Group's Motion To Compel Responses from Plaintiffs 640 Octavia LLC and Edward Kountze. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Supplemental responses to be served by May 28, 2021. Sanctions in the amount of $2,500 awarded to Defendants and payable by May 28, 2021. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)
